rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No platform-capability assertions made — lecture frames subagent dispatch and chat comparison generically; both shipped Claude Code primitives.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (subagents, sessions).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Subagent invocation in prompt 1 matches shipped Code behavior — single subagent dispatched with blinded read scope.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture body does not phrase any session-open instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label mandatory on factual claims.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The N=2 / N=5–10 / N=20–30 paragraph is a teaching estimate about sampling noise (generic statistical framing), not a sourced research claim. No citation required; ranges are deliberately fuzzy and pedagogical, not load-bearing numerical facts.
No round-number AI failure rates cited. Sampling-N teaching estimates are not zombie stats.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No practitioner citations in this lecture — no source-verification block required.

All three fenced blocks use concrete paths (`module-5/briefing-second-run.md`, `module-5/briefing.md`, etc.) and explicit references; no `[BRACKETS]`. Take-home prompt has Claude ask for filename instead of using a placeholder — matches §1 qualifier (Claude proposes/asks).
Three lead-ins present (lines 15, 39, 68), each a single sentence with command verb under 20 words.
All three prompt blocks use exact `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` form — renderer-compatible (matches `decoratePrompts` strong-text equal to 'Prompt' check, rule 28). No custom labels, no closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skill invocations in any prompt block.

Prompt 2 reads `module-5/briefing-second-run.md` — the exact deterministic path prompt 1 wrote — plus the briefing/claim-pool/adjudicated-claims chain from prior exercises. Back-references match files written in M5 spine.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-sample methods need structural enforcement (N, independence, variance).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: N=2 is named structurally (one subagent regenerates; trainer + subagent = two runs). Independence is enforced via blinded subagent read scope (must NOT read first briefing, claim-pool, scoreboard, detectors). Variance mechanism = subagent stochasticity. Body line 13 names that N=2 is demo-not-measurement and points at production N=20–30, satisfying the structural-honesty side of rule 8.
No factual citations inside the prompt blocks.

Prompt 1 names destination (`module-5/briefing-second-run.md`) — distinct path from first briefing, no overwrite needed. Prompt 3 has Claude ask for filename then write to `judges/<filename>` — explicit destination negotiated at runtime; no version-soup risk.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal hand-off + Claude self-augments for take-home prompts.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Take-home prompt (line 70-83) uses minimal hand-off shape: failure-mode list + adapt-to-output instruction + Claude asks for output/evidence/filename + sized constraint (under 20 lines + Known limit closer). Sized to fit ANY student output, as specified.
All file destinations are explicit subpaths: `module-5/briefing-second-run.md`, `judges/<filename>`. No naked filenames at root.

Prompt 1 uses `subagent` for blind regeneration (independence is the whole pedagogy of self-consistency). The independence requirement is named in the body (line 15: 'blind to the first run') so the design rationale is explicit without an extra explanation sentence.

All three labels are exactly `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` with the destination chip in italic parens. `decoratePrompts` will match strong-text='Prompt' and render header chip + Copy button correctly across all three.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Spawn one subagent to generate a second briefing
regenerates the briefing from the same evidence surface, blind to the first run
Show me the comparison in chat
write the judge as a markdown file at that path
